_comment: GENERAL, domain-agnostic house-style colour tokens. Shared by ALL diagrams, lives in the skill (one source of truth). A diagram does NOT define colours; it MAPS its own domains onto the generic category slots below (cat1..cat6). Two consumers: SVG generators load this JSON (see presentation .py files) and use only these values — never a literal hex; D2-native diagrams import the generated palette.d2 (run build-style.py after editing this). 'neutral' covers everything non-categorical (text, edges, surfaces, borders) so no diagram file needs its own greys either.
_roles_comment: Named SEMANTIC roles (vs the generic cat slots). build-style.py generates these into EVERY tool: D2 (style.d2, with shape), Mermaid (palette.mmd/.css), PlantUML (palette.puml stereotype + palette.c4.puml tag) — colour everywhere; 'shape' only binds in D2 (in Mermaid/PlantUML the author picks the shape via node syntax/keyword, so shape here is a hint). Colour is either a 'slot' reference (cat/external, so it can never drift) or explicit fill/stroke/text. Optional: radius, sw (stroke-width), font-size.

svc.desc: service / process / running component (the default box)

actor.desc: a human or external role that initiates something

decision.desc: a branch point in a flow

source.desc: an input / data source (file set, spreadsheet, RO-Crate, feed)

process.desc: a transform / build step that emits an artefact

artefact.desc: a produced output (built site, bundle, report)

infra.desc: a hosting / infrastructure component (web server, CDN)

flow.desc: an internal data flow between build-time steps

human.desc: a person acting on something (maintain, set up, review)

publish.desc: a bold cross-boundary publish / hand-off (deploy, deposit)

_state_machine_comment: State-machine vocabulary, one colour source for all three tools. D2 has no state grammar, so build-style.py generates state-machine.d2 (importable classes carrying the initial-dot / final-double-border / choice-diamond conventions). Mermaid (stateDiagram-v2) and PlantUML (state) have NATIVE grammars where [*] gives initial/final; there these colours arrive via the generated classDef / stereotype. Editing these recolours state machines everywhere. See state-machines.md.

start.desc: initial pseudostate — small solid dot (empty label)

final.desc: final state — solid dot with a double-border ring

choice.desc: a branch / choice pseudostate (put the action on the outgoing edges)

composite.desc: a state containing a nested machine (container)
